title: Houses for Rent in Bedford Heights, OH
metaTitle: Houses for Rent in Bedford Heights, Ohio
metaDescription: Looking for houses for rent in Bedford Heights, Ohio? See its location southeast of Cleveland, rent benchmarks, and how to tell us what you need.
primaryKeyword: houses for rent Bedford Heights ohio
answerBox: Bedford Heights is a Cuyahoga County suburb southeast of Cleveland, near the city of Bedford. Rent Finder Cleveland's rental homes are currently concentrated in Cleveland proper rather than Bedford Heights, but tell us what you're looking for and we'll show you comparable voucher-friendly homes we manage nearby.

sections.h2: Renting a house in Bedford Heights, Ohio
body: Bedford Heights is a Cuyahoga County suburb in the southeast part of the county, sitting alongside the separate city of Bedford. Like many of the county's southeast suburbs, it developed as a residential community outside Cleveland's city limits, with its own municipal government distinct from both Cleveland and neighboring Bedford.

For renters comparing Bedford Heights to city-of-Cleveland options, it helps to know the two markets sit under different jurisdictions: Bedford Heights is governed as its own suburb within Cuyahoga County, while neighborhoods like Lee-Harvard, Mount Pleasant, and Union-Miles Park a few miles northwest are part of the City of Cleveland itself, with Cleveland's own municipal rules, inspection requirements, and (where applicable) Lead-Safe Certification obligations for older rental housing.

**Rent Finder Cleveland manages 90+ voucher-friendly rental homes concentrated in Cleveland's east and southeast side neighborhoods, and Bedford Heights isn't currently part of our portfolio.** If a Bedford Heights address specifically is a requirement, a suburb-focused property manager will have more direct listings there. If your search is flexible on the exact suburb, [tell us what you're looking for](/p/book-showing) — bedrooms, budget, and voucher status — and we can point you to comparable homes we manage in nearby Cleveland neighborhoods like Lee-Harvard, Mount Pleasant, or Union-Miles.

sections.h2: What does it cost to rent a house near Bedford Heights?
body: Bedford Heights-specific rents vary by house and condition, but as a Greater Cleveland-area benchmark, Zumper's July 4, 2026 report shows a metro median of about **$1,100** for a 2-bedroom and **$1,350** for a 3-bedroom home. Within our own nearby Cleveland southeast-side portfolio, 2-bedroom rental homes typically run **$750–$1,100** a month and 3-bedroom homes typically run **$1,000–$1,500**, based on our real current listings in that part of the city.

headers: Typical rent (our nearby Cleveland SE-side portfolio)

sections.h2: Getting around Bedford Heights and southeast Cuyahoga County
body: Bedford Heights and the neighboring city of Bedford sit in southeast Cuyahoga County, in the same general corridor as Cleveland's Lee-Harvard, Mount Pleasant, and Union-Miles Park neighborhoods. As with much of eastern Cuyahoga County, this part of the region falls within Northeast Ohio's lake-effect snow belt, which typically sees more seasonal snowfall than west-side, lakeshore-adjacent areas.

items: **Location:** Southeast Cuyahoga County, adjacent to the city of Bedford
**Nearby Cleveland neighborhoods:** Lee-Harvard, Mount Pleasant, Union-Miles Park
**Climate note:** Part of Northeast Ohio's east-side snow belt
**Region:** Southeast Cuyahoga County, within the same general corridor as several of our managed Cleveland neighborhoods

body: Ohio has no statewide law requiring landlords to accept Housing Choice Vouchers; acceptance depends on the individual property owner unless a specific municipality has adopted its own protection ordinance. Bedford Heights is not among the Cuyahoga County suburbs reported to have a local source-of-income protection ordinance, so voucher holders should confirm directly with any specific landlord there.

**Every home Rent Finder Cleveland manages accepts Section 8 / Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready**, including our nearby homes in Cleveland's southeast-side neighborhoods. The [Cuyahoga Metropolitan Housing Authority (CMHA)](https://www.cmha.net/housing/applicants/housing_choice_vouchers.php) administers the Housing Choice Voucher program countywide, covering both Cleveland and its suburbs including Bedford Heights, and accepts preliminary applications year-round. Our [Section 8 housing guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) covers the local process in more depth.

If your search includes Cleveland proper, our [houses for rent in Cleveland](/houses-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) pillar page covers the southeast-side neighborhoods where we manage inventory nearby. You can also compare notes on the separate city of [Bedford](/cleveland-rentals/suburbs/houses-for-rent-bedford-ohio/) and [Warrensville Heights](/cleveland-rentals/suburbs/houses-for-rent-warrensville-heights-ohio/), or browse our full [suburb-by-suburb guide](/cleveland-rentals/suburbs/) to see everywhere we cover.

faq.q: Does Rent Finder Cleveland manage houses in Bedford Heights, Ohio?
faq.a: Not currently. Our 90+ voucher-friendly rental homes are concentrated in Cleveland's east and southeast side neighborhoods. Bedford Heights isn't part of our current portfolio, but tell us what you're looking for and we'll point you to comparable homes we manage nearby in Cleveland.

faq.q: How much does it cost to rent a house near Bedford Heights, Ohio?
faq.a: In our nearby Cleveland southeast-side portfolio, 2-bedroom rental homes typically run $750–$1,100 a month and 3-bedroom homes run $1,000–$1,500. As a broader metro benchmark, Zumper's July 2026 report shows a median of about $1,100 for 2-bedroom and $1,350 for 3-bedroom units.

faq.q: Is Bedford Heights the same as Bedford, Ohio?
faq.a: No. Bedford Heights and Bedford are two separate, adjacent Cuyahoga County suburbs southeast of Cleveland, each with its own municipal government.

faq.q: Do landlords in Bedford Heights accept Section 8 vouchers?
faq.a: It depends on the individual landlord — Ohio has no statewide law requiring voucher acceptance, and Bedford Heights isn't reported to have a local source-of-income protection ordinance. Every home Rent Finder Cleveland manages accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready; CMHA administers the voucher program countywide, including Bedford Heights.
